source_name: UNCW academic and health program cycle
topic: Healthcare students: confirm whether you need BLS before clinicals
audience: UNCW-area nursing, pre-health, and healthcare students
relevance_reason: Healthcare students often need BLS Provider training before clinical rotations, onboarding, or program milestones.
course_angle: AHA BLS Provider for healthcare students and providers
draft_seed: Healthcare program requirements can be specific. If your paperwork says BLS Provider, a general CPR card may not be the right match.

topic: CFCC-area health science students: CPR vs BLS reminder
audience: CFCC-area health science, dental, EMS, and allied health students
relevance_reason: Local health science programs create seasonal demand for correctly matched CPR/BLS credentials.
course_angle: AHA BLS Provider or renewal options
draft_seed: Before booking a class, check whether your program asks for BLS Provider rather than general CPR.

topic: Coastal preparedness includes first aid before help arrives
audience: coastal NC families, churches, workplaces, and neighborhood groups
relevance_reason: Storm planning is a recurring coastal topic, and emergency response can be delayed by weather or road conditions.
course_angle: First Aid/CPR/AED readiness for the first few minutes
draft_seed: A storm kit is more useful when someone nearby also knows basic first aid, CPR, and AED response.

topic: Pool season: CPR and AED readiness for neighborhoods
audience: HOA boards, pool committees, community managers, and neighborhood volunteers
relevance_reason: Pool season raises community interest in drowning prevention, CPR, AED access, and first response planning.
course_angle: Heartsaver First Aid CPR AED for community responders
draft_seed: Before the pool gets busy, review who can start CPR, who knows where the AED is, and who can help while 911 is on the way.

topic: Church safety teams: AED and Sunday morning readiness
audience: church safety teams, ushers, greeters, and children's ministry volunteers
relevance_reason: Faith communities routinely gather large mixed-age groups and benefit from clear first response roles.
course_angle: Group CPR/AED and First Aid/CPR/AED training
draft_seed: If your church has an AED or a safety team, make sure the plan is understood before a Sunday morning emergency.

source_name: OSHA medical and first aid readiness
topic: Workplace first aid kits need trained responders too
audience: small businesses, supervisors, safety coordinators, and front desk teams
relevance_reason: Workplaces often have first aid supplies but fewer people trained to use them in time-sensitive emergencies.
course_angle: HSI First Aid CPR AED or group workplace training
draft_seed: A stocked first aid kit is helpful, but a trained person standing near it is better.

topic: Parents and grandparents: practice infant choking response
audience: parents, grandparents, babysitters, and new families
relevance_reason: Infant choking response is a recurring caregiver concern and is best practiced before an emergency.
draft_seed: Infant choking is not something families want to figure out for the first time during an emergency.

source_name: Summer camp and childcare safety season
topic: Camp and childcare teams: summer CPR and pediatric first aid readiness
audience: daycare staff, camp staff, youth program leaders, and childcare teams
relevance_reason: Summer programs increase youth activity, heat exposure, water exposure, field trips, and first aid needs.
draft_seed: Summer youth programs move fast; pediatric first aid and CPR practice helps teams respond with a plan.
